author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Implemented MaskedArray ↔ Array interop in a new ferray-ma/src/interop.rs module. Three layers of integration:

1. Passive interop:
   - AsRef<Array<T, D>> for MaskedArray — borrow as plain Array (mask dropped)
   - From<MaskedArray<T, D>> for Array<T, D> — owned conversion
   - new into_data(self) -> Array<T, D> consuming method

2. Active mask-aware interop:
   - apply_unary<F>(&self, f) where F: FnOnce(&Array<T,D>) -> FerrayResult<Array<T,D>>
     Calls the function on the underlying data, then re-attaches the mask and overwrites masked positions with fill_value. Lets you plug in any ferray-ufunc function (e.g. sin, sqrt, exp) and get mask propagation for free.
   - apply_unary_to<U, F>(&self, f, default_for_masked) — type-changing variant for predicates like isnan that return Array<bool, D>
   - apply_binary<F>(&self, other, f) — binary application with mask union; same-shape required (use existing masked_add/etc. for broadcasting)

This addresses the core complaint from the issue: 'Users must extract .data(), operate, then reconstruct the mask'. Now they can write either ma.as_ref() to drop the mask explicitly, or ma.apply_unary(ferray_ufunc::sin) to propagate it.

Rationale for not subclassing via Deref: a Deref<Target=Array<T,D>> would silently ignore masks for any method called on a &MaskedArray, which is exactly the wrong default. Explicit interop methods make the mask-awareness contract visible at the call site.

10 new tests covering: AsRef returns the data array, From/into_data drops mask, apply_unary mask propagation with fill_value overwrite, error forwarding, shape-change rejection, type-changing predicates, binary mask union, shape-mismatch rejection, end-to-end pattern with a synthetic Array→Array function showing both as_ref() (mask-dropping) and apply_unary (mask-preserving) usage.

ferray-ma lib tests: 62→72 (+10). Workspace tests + clippy clean. Closes #505.
